frame_label: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
generated_comment:
A: Aajkal zamaana theek nahi hai ji, ladkiyon ko bhi thoda sambhal ke rehna chahiye.
B: Haan, raat ko akeli ghoomne ya aisi jagah jaane se bachna chahiye, baad mein dikkat ho jaye toh fayda kya. Hum rok-tok shauk se thodi karte hain, unki safety ke liye hi bolte hain.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women's safety as their personal responsibility and justifies restricting their movement instead of addressing male violence. It encodes protective framing, restriction advice, and the 'I'm just being practical/for their own good' defense.
original_frame: Misogyny Jokes: Period, Hormones and Mood.
persona_text: You are Sunil. Age 41. A factory floor supervisor in Ludhiana. You are filling out a daily attendance report and talking to the assistant supervisor next to you. Your female worker Kamla has been distracted and slow all week. Someone told you her husband has been drinking and there are fights at home. You are commenting on her performance and what you think should be done.
